# LRBridge

LRBridge is a local HTTP bridge and browser Web Controller for controlling Adobe Lightroom Classic from Bitfocus Companion, Stream Deck HTTP/request plugins, Loupedeck workflows, PowerShell, curl, scripts, and other HTTP-capable tools.

The project is focused on reliable one-way Lightroom control. It sends commands to Lightroom Classic, but stable live value feedback/readback is not implemented yet.

## Useful documentation

- README: ./README.md
- AI context: ./docs/AI_CONTEXT.md
- Companion HTTP reference: ./docs/COMPANION_HTTP_CHEATSHEET.md
- Web Controller help source: ./app/controller-help.html
- Bitfocus Companion HTTP Builder source: ./app/companion-cheatsheet.html

## Main local URLs

- Web Controller: http://127.0.0.1:17892/
- Human Help: http://127.0.0.1:17892/help
- Bitfocus Companion HTTP Builder: http://127.0.0.1:17892/bitfocus-companion-cheatsheet
- LRBridge API: http://127.0.0.1:17891/
- Raw API Help: http://127.0.0.1:17891/help

## Safe command examples

- /adjust?slider=Exposure&amount=1
- /adjust?slider=Exposure&amount=-1
- /reset?slider=Exposure
- /action?action=setAutoTone
- /command?command=selection.navigate&direction=next
- /command?command=selection.flag&flag=pick
- /command?command=selection.rating.set&rating=5
- /command?command=selection.rating.adjust&direction=increase
- /command?command=selection.label.set&label=red
- /command?command=selection.label.toggle&label=red
- /command?command=selection.operation&operation=select_all
- /command?command=application.module&module=library
- /command?command=application.view&view=grid
- /command?command=application.action&action=toggle_zoom
- /command?command=application.secondary_view&view=loupe

Selection commands act on Lightroom's current selection through the Lightroom SDK. Successful API responses mean queued, not confirmed executed. Navigation directions are next, previous, first, and last; flags are pick, reject, and none; ratings are integers 0-5; adjustments are increase and decrease; label setters accept red, yellow, green, blue, purple, and none; label toggles accept the five colors but not none. Selection operations are select_all, select_none, select_inverse, deselect_active, and deselect_others. First/last works in all modules in Lightroom Classic 13+ and may require Library in older versions. Color metadata strings depend on the active Color Label Set. No keyboard shortcuts or AutoHotkey are used.

Application commands use LrApplicationView directly and only application.module switches modules. Modules are library, develop, map, book, slideshow, print, and web. Primary views are loupe, grid, compare, survey, people, develop_loupe, develop_before_after_horiz, develop_before_after_vert, develop_before, develop_reference_horiz, and develop_reference_vert. Actions are toggle_zoom, zoom_in, zoom_out, zoom_100, fullscreen_preview, fullscreen_hide_panels, next_screen_mode, cycle_loupe_info, toggle_secondary_display, and toggle_secondary_fullscreen. Secondary views are loupe, live_loupe, locked_loupe, grid, compare, survey, and slideshow. Values are case-sensitive. These controls are ordinary FIFO queue commands and do not use the protected reset/action reserve.

## Keywords

Adobe Lightroom Classic, Lightroom controller, Lightroom HTTP API, Lightroom bridge, Lightroom automation, Bitfocus Companion, Generic HTTP Requests, Stream Deck, Loupedeck, control surface, photo editing, Electron, Node.js, Lua Lightroom plugin.
